text: Using incognito chats | Claude Help Center Using incognito chats Incognito chats are available to all Claude users (Free, Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans). What are incognito chats? Incognito chats are temporary conversations that aren't saved to your chat history or to Claude’s memory . These differ from regular chats in several ways: Incognito chats are not used for training. See our Privacy Center for more information: Is my data used for model training? (for Free, Pro, and Max plans) Is my data used for model training? (for Team and Enterprise plans) Incognito chats are not used for training. See our Privacy Center for more information: Is my data used for model training? (for Free, Pro, and Max plans) Is my data used for model training? (for Free, Pro, and Max plans) Is my data used for model training? (for Team and Enterprise plans) Is my data used for model training? (for Team and Enterprise plans) While incognito chats aren't saved to your chat history, they are retained for either 30 days (default), or longer in accordance with your organization's custom data retention setting (available for Enterprise plans). While incognito chats aren't saved to your chat history, they are retained for either 30 days (default), or longer in accordance with your organization's custom data retention setting (available for Enterprise plans). Claude won’t pull information from incognito chats when searching previous conversations. Claude won’t pull information from incognito chats when searching previous conversations. If you are using Claude’s memory: Starting an incognito chat won’t use Claude’s existing memory. Incognito chats will not be included in future memory summaries. If you are using Claude’s memory: Starting an incognito chat won’t use Claude’s existing memory. Starting an incognito chat won’t use Claude’s existing memory. Incognito chats will not be included in future memory summaries. Incognito chats will not be included in future memory summaries. How to start an incognito chat When starting a new chat with Claude outside of a project, you'll see a ghost icon in the upper right corner of your screen: Click the ghost icon to enable incognito mode. Click the ghost icon to enable incognito mode. The interface will indicate you're in an incognito chat with a black border and “Incognito chat” label in the upper left corner.
